subject: Cloud Computing
title: Open Source vs. Cloud Tension
description: A fast breakdown of Open Source vs. Cloud Tension for builders.

hook.text: MongoDB, Elastic, and Redis built popular open-source databases. Then AWS sold them as services.

buildup.text: Cloud providers took free software, hosted it, and charged customers to use it.

discovery.text: Original creators saw their revenue threatened by companies running their own code for profit.

twist.text: Many projects changed their licenses to block cloud providers. The community split over it.

climax.text: The debate rages: is cloud killing open source, or helping it thrive at massive scale?

punchline.text: Free software meets billion-dollar clouds. It's complicated.

quiz.question: Why did some open-source projects change their licenses?
options: To prevent cloud providers from profiting off their free software
To make their software proprietary and charge for it
To comply with new government regulations on software
